description_long: This is a temporary construction or safety fence featuring a wooden or metal frame with two vertical posts and a mesh wire panel spanning the width. The lower section of the fence is reinforced with a solid rectangular panel painted in a high-visibility yellow and black diagonal hazard stripe pattern, commonly used to signal restricted or dangerous areas. The mesh is a diamond-patterned wire grid, providing visibility while preventing passage. The posts are slightly angled outward, giving the structure added stability. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and safety over aesthetics, and is typical for urban construction sites or roadwork zones. There are no visible gates, hinges, or decorative elements beyond the hazard panel.
description: A safety fence with a yellow-black hazard-striped lower panel, diamond mesh wire, and sturdy wooden or metal posts, designed for construction or restricted urban areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full mesh panel and the prominent yellow-black hazard-striped lower section.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the depth of the posts, with no mesh or panel details visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the mesh and hazard-striped panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the posts without mesh or panel details.
